message:
feat(core,abi): port SHA-512/SHA-256/MD5 crypt to core::crypt (bd-shc-epic)

Lifts ~353 lines of pure RFC-defined crypto out of unistd_abi.rs into
core::crypt: the crypt(3)-style base-64 encoder, the Ulrich Drepper
SHA-crypt algorithms ($5$ and $6$), and the Poul-Henning Kamp MD5-crypt
algorithm ($1$).

New core surface:
  pub mod base64    // ALPHABET, encode(input, n_chars)
  pub mod sha512    // sha512_crypt(key, salt_bytes) -> Option<String>
  pub mod sha256    // sha256_crypt(...)
  pub mod md5       // md5_crypt(...) — MAX_MD5_SALT = 8

Added sha2 + md-5 as core deps (already workspace deps for abi).

Behavior preservation: the historical SHA-512 final-hash byte
transposition table is kept byte-identical to the existing abi inline
implementation. Three slot indices in that table differ from Drepper's
published spec by 1 — flagged for future investigation rather than
fixed in this port to avoid changing observable hash output.

Side-fix in base64::encode: early-exit on n_chars==0 prevents a u32
overflow that the previous abi inline impl had (it accumulated bits
without ever draining them, eventually shifting beyond u32 width).
Fixed because it surfaced under property-test coverage.

abi changes:
  - Drop CRYPT_B64, crypt_b64_encode, parse_crypt_salt shim, and the
    three crypt_sha512/sha256/md5 inline impls (~353 lines).
  - Replace with three 3-line shim functions that delegate to core.
    The crypt() entry point dispatcher is unchanged.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-shc-2):
  - 50 new core::crypt tests pass (8 base64 + 7 SHA-512 + 7 SHA-256
    + 8 MD5 + the 20 existing salt parser tests)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-shc-epic.

files.patch:
@@ -1,10 +1,14 @@
-//! `<crypt.h>` — password-hash building blocks (salt parser today).
+//! `<crypt.h>` — password-hash building blocks.
 //!
-//! Pure-safe Rust port of the byte-level prefix logic that previously
-//! lived inline in frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs::parse_crypt_salt.
-//! The actual SHA-256/SHA-512/MD5/DES key-stretching loops still live
-//! in the abi alongside the corresponding crypt() entry points; this
-//! module covers the format-specific parsing piece that's reusable
-//! and trivial to test in isolation.
+//! Pure-safe Rust port of the salt prefix parsing, crypt(3)-style
+//! base-64 encoding, and the SHA-512 / SHA-256 / MD5 password hashing
+//! algorithms. Each lived inline in frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs
+//! before being lifted here. The abi `crypt()` entry point is a thin
+//! shim that dispatches on the salt prefix and packs the result into
+//! a thread-local `*mut c_char` buffer.
 
+pub mod base64;
+pub mod md5;
 pub mod salt;
+pub mod sha256;
+pub mod sha512;
